meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Master Debugging
content.seo_description: Learn to master Claude Code and Antigravity to solve complex debugging issues. An advanced checklist for students building their first real-world SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform your CLI into an autonomous debugging engine. This setup allows you to identify root causes, simulate edge cases, and apply surgical fixes directly to your codebase, effectively turning your terminal into a senior developer's workstation.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the terminal spits out a red wall of text, and your momentum grinds to a halt. You’ve reached the threshold every developer knows: the 'black box' bug where the logic defies your current understanding. It’s not just a code error; it’s a test of your resolve to move from amateur to architect.

But what if you didn't have to debug alone? By mastering Claude Code and Antigravity, you are no longer just a student writing lines; you are a builder orchestrating an autonomous intelligence to solve your most stubborn architectural bottlenecks. This is how you reclaim your flow and ship code that actually works.

steps.title: Initialize Claude Code in your Project Root
steps.description: Establish a secure bridge between your local repository and Claude’s reasoning engine to allow deep context awareness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and create a comprehensive index of dependencies and core logic flow to prepare for debugging.

steps.title: Configure Antigravity Context Mapping
steps.description: Map your environment variables and specific file paths to ensure the AI understands the constraints of your SaaS architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the relationship between my API routes and the database schema. Highlight potential race conditions.

steps.title: Execute Targeted Error Diagnostics
steps.description: Feed the stack trace into Claude Code to get a structural breakdown of the failure rather than a generic guess.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace this error back to the origin point in the service layer. Explain the failure in terms of state mutation.

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases with Antigravity
steps.description: Use synthetic data generation to test if the fix actually addresses the edge case or just masks the symptom.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for this module that specifically targets the failure scenario identified in the logs.

steps.title: Implement Atomic Fixes
steps.description: Apply the generated patch through the CLI, ensuring your code remains clean and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the logic to be immutable and thread-safe. Apply the changes to the relevant files.

pro_tips: Always use the --trace flag in Claude Code to force the model to show its reasoning chain before writing code.
Map your project documentation as a 'system context' file so Claude understands your architectural intent vs. your current implementation.
Never accept the first patch; ask Claude to 'explain the trade-offs of this fix' before applying it to your main branch.
Use Antigravity to generate 'negative tests'—code designed to break your app—to find bugs before they happen in production.
Maintain a separate 'Claude-Memory' file in your repo that tracks recurring architectural patterns to speed up future debugging sessions.
content.next_steps: Once your first bug is squashed, document the pattern in your personal developer journal and start integrating automated CI/CD checks to prevent regression.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to dismantle any bug and build with professional-grade precision. Don't let a terminal error stop your journey to becoming a full-stack engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
